prompt: I want to apply a design token colour as a background on a custom div that's not a hearth Box component, and also use a token value as padding in a React inline style on a third-party chart component. What's the correct way to access hearth tokens for each case?

assertions.text: Recommends CSS variables for the custom div case
assertions.check: Response recommends using CSS custom properties (var(--h-color-*) or var(--h-spacing-*)) for styling a plain CSS class on the custom div

assertions.text: Recommends hearth-tokens/browser for the inline style case
assertions.check: Response recommends importing from @utilitywarehouse/hearth-tokens/browser (not the root import) for React inline styles

assertions.text: Does not recommend root hearth-tokens import for inline styles
assertions.check: Response does not suggest using 'import { ... } from @utilitywarehouse/hearth-tokens' (without /browser) for inline React styles

assertions.text: Explains why the two approaches differ
assertions.check: Response explains the distinction between the CSS variable approach and the JS token approach, or why root hearth-tokens is not for browser use
